(EZ-72) Support split bootstrap in configuration

This commit adds support to ezbake for splitting a project's bootstrap files
between /etc/puppetlabs/{project}/services.d/ and
/opt/puppetlabs/server/apps/{project}/config/services.d/

This serves the ultimate goal of putting user-configurable service entries
in one location, and the services that should not be modified in another.

It accomplishes

(PE-5211) Reorder dependencies for pe-console-services

because apparently order matters when resolving dependencies? So, for
consistency with other projects in this package, order is: 1) clojure libs, 2)
third party libs, 3) puppet labs libs, and alphabetical within each section.

(EZ-97) Add missing check for pid status after kill in aborted start

This commit adds one extra  check to the logic in the
start script which confirms whether an attempt to kill the Java process
for an aborted start was successful.  Without this check, an erroneous
"Java process not killed" trace message might be written even if the
process actually was killed.

(EZ-100) Terminate java process if start script times out

This commit adds logic to the start script to terminate the Java
background process and purge the associated pidfile if the service
has not been started by the time the start timeout has been reached.

commit refactors those functions into one single function that takes a
keyword to lookup as an extra parameter. It also updates the calls

Add :classpath-jars and :project-files features

When building for FIPS, we need to include the Bouncycastle FIPS jars in
the package, but the code can not be included in the uberjar since they
are signed jars. Therefore, we need to be able to pull down the version
we are building against and then put them in the package.

To do this, we pull the particular jars we are building against directly

spec, we were trying to install the bin script even if that
condition hadn't been met.  This commit should fix that issue,
and allow RPMs to be built for projects that don'

(EZ-112) Don't start puppetserver until networking comes online
pe-puppetserver has a dependency on the network.target, which ensures that the network is started prior to starting the pe-puppetserver. However, the pe-puppetserver really needs the network to be online prior to starting if it has to do any DNS lookups. (EZ-97) Kill background Java process if start script terminated

This commit will trap HUP, INT, and TERM signals sent to the start
script.  If any is trapped, the associated Java process that the start
script spawned will be terminated.  This is done so that if a 'restart'
is issued while a start is already in process, the Java process for the
first start attempt will be cleaned up before the sec

It's not required. But in case my hack here doesn't catch the java bin, or we are on a platform where we don't have an explicit java version, we end up with /usr/bin/java and that works for 80% of the situations.

I gave this a quick local test and it's working fine. However I have no time right now to kick of the acceptance tests with that. It would be great if a few people could test this and provide feedback.

(PE-6748) Update trapperkeeper-webserver-jetty9 dependency for pe-console-services

Bump to 0.9.0 of tk-w-j9 which has a fix for the poodle sslv3 security issue.

Fix some version strings for compatibility

The packaging tools were making some regex-y assumptions
about how we'd format our version strings.  My previous
stuff for SNAPSHOT versions did not comply with these
assumptions; this commit should hopefully fix that.

(EZ-15) Replace hard-coded path names.

This commit has a number of effects, including:

* Remove hard-coded path names in favor of functions that take path fragements
  and return File objects.
* Use clojure.java.io to access template resources which enables ezbake to be
  run from the git repository as a leiningen app OR as a plugin whose resources
  are stored in its jarfile.

(MAINT) Remove du

(CPR-401) Require 'which'

We add the dependency specifically on '/usr/bin/which' to be flexible
with minimal installers which may provide functionality from different
packages.

(EZ-97) Add comment about $! to start script

This commit adds a comment about why $! is used in the start script.

(SERVER-344) Choose best method to become puppet

Previously, only the su command was used to switch to the puppet user
and launch Puppet server in the foreground. This does not work on some
systems using SELinux. This commit attempts to use the runuser command
instead if it is installed and the user is running as root, otherwise
sudo or su is used.

(PE-7614) Fix SLES-11 pe-puppetserver init script

Without this patch the pe-puppetserver service in PE fails to start on SLES-11.

The root cause of the issue is that the JAVA_ARGS string contains
sub-strings which are intended to be positional arguments.  Because the
complete string is used when invoking a command, BASH expands the string
into positional arguments without considering the inner q

Add JAVA_BIN to systemd service files

The EnvironmentFile paths are config locations so if a user ever
modified them, they're not replaced. By defining this in the service
file, the packaging will update the location. The result is that even
if a java location changes that a simple yum/apt upgrade will respect
the new value in most situations.

Signed-off-by: Tim Meusel <tim.de>

if a java location changes that a simple yum/apt upgrade will respect
the new value in most situations.
